relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul and Timothy write as servants to the holy ones in Philippi.
Grace and peace open the partnership.
Every remembrance becomes thanksgiving.
Prayer for them is made with joy.
Their partnership in the good news has endured from the first day.
The Divine who began the good work will complete it.
They share grace with Paul in chains and defense of the good news.
He longs for them with Messiah’s inward affection.
Love is prayed into abounding knowledge and discernment.
Discernment tests what truly matters.
Justice-fruit fills them through Jesus Messiah.
Paul’s chains have advanced the good news.
The whole guard knows his imprisonment is in Messiah.
Others grow bold because of his chains.
Some preach from rivalry; some from goodwill.
Love recognizes Paul’s appointment to defend the good news.
Selfish ambition tries to add pressure to his chains.
Still Messiah is proclaimed, and Paul rejoices.
Prayer and Spirit-supply turn pressure toward saving/healing.
His hope is that Messiah be magnified in his body.
To live is Messiah; to die is gain.
Life in flesh still means fruitful labor.
He is pulled between departure to Messiah and remaining.
Remaining in the body is needed for them.
He stays for their progress and joy in trust.
His presence will increase their Messiah-boast.
Live as good-news citizens, one Spirit and one soul.
Fearlessness before opposition signals Divine saving/healing.
Trust and suffering are both graced into the Messiah path.
They share the same struggle they saw and hear in Paul.

contemplative_note: Philippians 1 is joy under pressure. Chains become proclamation, love becomes discernment, and Messiah is magnified in the body whether by life or death.
